objects.name: Analytic 1737
objects.description: Correlates (1) application access to device- or environment-specific attributes used to validate target conditions, (2) suppression of sensitive behavior until those attributes match an expected value, and (3) immediate transition into protected actions such as sensor use, file access, or network communication only after the condition is satisfied. The defender observes a causal chain where an app repeatedly evaluates device state or environment context and withholds execution until a target-specific match occurs.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application holds permissions enabling environment validation (e.g., location, phone state, nearby device/network context) and subsequently delays protected activity until qualifying values are present

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application queries target-selection attributes (e.g., location, SIM/operator, locale, device state, network identity) and then conditionally invokes sensitive framework APIs only after expected value is observed

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between environment checks and subsequent guarded execution
Environment attributes treated as likely guardrail inputs, such as locale, geolocation, carrier, Wi-Fi identity, device model, or lock state
Amount of suppressed or low-activity runtime before sensitive behavior begins
Baseline of legitimate apps expected to evaluate environment attributes before conditional feature activation
Whether guarded execution is only suspicious when activated from background or without recent user interaction
Minimum outbound traffic volume used to distinguish meaningful guarded execution from benign telemetry
